violations.description: Thread.Sleep(50) inside synchronous file-IO retry helper TryWriteFaultFile blocks the test thread.
violations.suggestion: Convert TryWriteFaultFile to async and replace Thread.Sleep with await Task.Delay inside the bounded retry loop.

violations.description: Thread.Sleep(50) inside synchronous file-IO retry helper TryDeleteFaultFile blocks the test thread.
violations.suggestion: Convert TryDeleteFaultFile to async and replace Thread.Sleep with await Task.Delay inside the bounded retry loop.

violations.description: PERSISTENT REGRESSION (flagged 2026-04-18): Task.Delay(17s) used as a real-time wait for hosted-service capture instead of a fake clock.
violations.suggestion: Inject TimeProvider into DaprHealthHistoryCollector and use FakeTimeProvider.Advance(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(15)) to drive the timer deterministically.

violations.description: PERSISTENT REGRESSION (flagged 2026-04-18): Task.Delay(17s) waits on hosted-service tick in ExecuteAsync_SkipsWrite_WhenNoComponentsReturned.
violations.suggestion: Replace with FakeTimeProvider.Advance and a TaskCompletionSource signal from the collector when an iteration completes.

violations.description: PERSISTENT REGRESSION (flagged 2026-04-18): Task.Delay(17s) waits on hosted-service tick in ExecuteAsync_ContinuesOnWriteFailure.
violations.suggestion: Replace with FakeTimeProvider.Advance and a poll-with-deadline that observes infraService.Received().GetComponentsAsync.

violations.description: Task.Delay(2000) outside a polling loop while waiting for sidecar actor placement registration.
violations.suggestion: Replace with a bounded poll loop against a sidecar readiness endpoint (e.g. /v1.0/metadata or actor placement health) with a deadline.

violations.description: Task.Delay(2000) outside a polling loop while waiting for sidecar actor placement registration in Tenants integration fixture.
violations.suggestion: Replace with a bounded poll loop against the Dapr actor placement readiness endpoint with a deadline.

violations.description: Asserts ProcessedAt against DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddSeconds(-5) without an injected clock.
violations.suggestion: Inject TimeProvider into IdempotencyRecord.FromResult and assert against a fixed FakeTimeProvider.GetUtcNow() value.

violations.description: Asserts FailedAt > DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddMinutes(-1) using wall-clock comparison.
violations.suggestion: Inject TimeProvider into the dead-letter recording path and assert FailedAt equals a fixed FakeTimeProvider value.

violations.description: Asserts FailedAt <= DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddSeconds(1); flaky under GC pause or slow CI agent.
violations.suggestion: Use FakeTimeProvider so the recorded FailedAt equals a known fixed instant; replace the inequality with ShouldBe.

violations.description: Task.Delay(100) outside a polling loop while waiting for hosted-service to evaluate disabled flag.
violations.suggestion: Replace with a TaskCompletionSource signaled by the collector or a poll-with-deadline asserting infraService.DidNotReceive().

recommendations: Inject TimeProvider into DaprHealthHistoryCollector and rewrite all four DaprHealthHistoryCollectorTests to use Microsoft.Extensions.Time.Testing.FakeTimeProvider with explicit Advance(TimeSpan) calls; this single change eliminates 4 of 11 violations and removes 51 seconds of wall-clock wait per test run (PERSISTENT REGRESSION since 2026-04-18).

Convert PubSubDeliveryProofTests.TryWriteFaultFile / TryDeleteFaultFile to async (TryWriteFaultFileAsync) and use await Task.Delay(50) inside the bounded retry loop to remove the two synchronous Thread.Sleep calls.

Replace the bare Task.Delay(2000) sidecar-warmup waits in DaprTestContainerFixture.cs:104 and TenantsDaprTestFixture.cs:101 with a bounded poll against /v1.0/metadata or the actor placement readiness endpoint, capping at the existing HealthTimeoutSeconds budget.

Inject TimeProvider into IdempotencyRecord and the dead-letter recorder, then rewrite the three wall-clock assertions in IdempotencyRecordTests and DeadLetterMessageCompletenessTests to use FakeTimeProvider.GetUtcNow() and ShouldBe equality rather than inequality windows.

Add an .editorconfig rule (dotnet_diagnostic.HEXA_TEST_NoThreadSleep.severity = error) and a banned-API analyzer entry to fail the build when Thread.Sleep or Task.Delay >= 1s appears in tests/ outside designated polling helpers, preventing regression of the persistent DaprHealthHistoryCollector pattern.
summary: The .NET test suite has substantial determinism risk concentrated in two persistent hot spots: DaprHealthHistoryCollectorTests still uses three 17-second real-time waits that were already flagged in the prior 2026-04-18 review, and three wall-clock UtcNow assertions can flake on slow CI. The score floors at 0 because the 10 HIGH violations exceed the 100-point budget; remediation should prioritize injecting TimeProvider into DaprHealthHistoryCollector, IdempotencyRecord, and the dead-letter recorder.
